title: Unit 00s eyes
wikitext:
[]: Unit-00's eye(s)             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Feb 10, 2014 7:07 pm So, upon perusal of the wiki, I found this. UrsusArctos wrote: Wow, Eva-00 does have two eyes! Fufufu.

[]: Re: Unit-00's eye(s)             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Feb 10, 2014 9:39 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Maybe 00 really does have two eyes and the big mechanical thing is just feeding data. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Stratomeister » Mon Feb 10, 2014 10:17 pm The mechanism must be some sort of optical lens, either feeding information directly to the pilot's display, or to the Eva itself. I find it very unlikely that Unit-00 two eyes could be squeezed into that minuscule area. On a related note, are there any monitor displays for the other Evangelions showing similar data? Specifically one for Unit-02, displaying information for her four eyes?

[]: Re: Unit-00's eye(s)             Quote Post by Electreel » Thu Feb 13, 2014 2:47 pm It should be noted that Zerogōki was initially equiped with 5 camera lenses:

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 13, 2014 2:50 pm It appears that Unit Zero has a complex visual system and sensor package and the other Evas just stick lenses right over their biological eyes. I love how that invites speculation. Maybe the Eva keeps trying to "heal" the places where the sensors and cameras are wired into its brain, so they dropped it on the production models? Then again, the MPEs don't even appear to have eyes. Maybe they use radar.

[]:             Quote Post by Electreel » Thu Feb 13, 2014 3:17 pm Chuckman wrote: Maybe they use radar.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 13, 2014 3:38 pm Maybe they can see Asuka's soul. That's why they're so licky. They're soul lickers.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Feb 13, 2014 9:13 pm That huge "eye" is probably a fisheye lens, designed to broaden the Eva's field of view. Eva-00's head doesn't look all that narrow, it's just the helmet design that shrinks down the faceplate to a very small size. Why is Eva-00 given a cyclops face when the other Evas all have their original eye pairs beats me. I guess they were trying to hide the Eva's true appearance, and then decided that this was too tacky an arrangement and chose to leave the eyes mostly exposed and focus on style and gaudy color schemes to hide the appearance of the thing underneath.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 13, 2014 9:25 pm I think experimenting with different sensor packages and camera systems is a little more compelling than Nerv trying to make the Evas look cool. :p It is a prototype, after all. They'd want to experiment with it and see what works best.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Feb 13, 2014 9:56 pm Of course they'd experiment with the prototype, but that's the only explanation I have for why the eyes and jaw in this Eva are completely hidden. Even Eva-02 had moving eye plates and it obviously had four eyes! With this Eva we had to look at a blink-and-you-miss-it listing to see that it had two eyes inside that helmet.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 13, 2014 9:57 pm Maybe Evas have bad breath.

[]:             Quote Post by Stratomeister » Thu Feb 13, 2014 11:42 pm Unit-00 is just having a hard time expressing itself, she just wants to be different from the other Evas. She did end up changing her outfit in the first third of the series, and boy did that colour coordinations and those shoulder pylons work wonders!

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Feb 13, 2014 11:44 pm Either that, or the entire listing is a result of questionable production values. (The discussion quote was made by me in December 2007 . It's old .) Also notice that Eva-00, the only Eva that has the listing, seems to have human male genitalia and no core, which is weird. If Eva-00 has some completely different sort of ocular arrangement under the helmet, it wouldn't be too surprising. (Yeah, I just read through the entire discussion and realized just how wonky it sounds in context. Plus, we now have Eva-08 and Mark.09, so multi-eyed Evas are still a possibility.)

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Feb 13, 2014 11:47 pm I don't want to know what the genitals are for.

[]:             Quote Post by Stratomeister » Thu Feb 13, 2014 11:52 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Either that, or the entire listing is a result of questionable production values.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Feb 14, 2014 4:11 am can we tone down the goofy one-liners, please? This is discussion, not chit-chat or off-topic.

[]: Re:             Quote Post by di0shi » Mon Dec 21, 2020 6:56 pm UrsusArctos wrote: ...Eva-00, the only Eva that has the listing, seems to have human male genitalia...

[]: Re: Unit-00's eye(s)             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Mon Dec 21, 2020 7:24 pm I confess I always thought 00 had one eye but with possible chameleon movement range. Which made sense to me as Rei is deeply methodical and all about the detail. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Unit-00's eye(s)             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 21, 2020 7:28 pm I see basically 2 explanations: a) They just copied a list of organs from somewhere and didn't screen it further, ie, this is unintentional. Then again, while you could imagine a bunch of animators not knowing about obscure glands, you'd think they would have caught the eyes. b) It's the prototype, we've seen that they had difficulty getting the EVAs into a proper humanoid shape, we've seen the misshapen limbs of the discarded prototypes, the next to the random number of eyes... Maybe it wasn't supposed to grow those glands but the genes just got expressed. Actually, you have all the genes for making both man and woman parts, what decides which you grow is a signal cascade started by a single gene on the Y chromosome. You can grow eggs from male stem cells and sperm from female ones (though sperm from reprogrammed woman cells would beget only daughters cause there is no Y chromosome) So it's probably a cell programming error. Likewise, the second eye might be tiny and vestigial. So without the helmet, zerogokis face looks like this: ".O" Also the "unit zero" Fuyutsuki is shown in ep 21 is probably a different prototype that ended up on the EVA graveyard

[]: Re: Re:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 22, 2020 2:48 am di0shi wrote: I made an entire account just so I could comment on this. What? Where does it say that 00 has human male genitals?
summary:
[]: Unit-00's eye(s)             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Feb 10, 2014 7:07 pm So, upon perusal of the wiki, I found this. UrsusArctos wrote: Wow, Eva-00 does have two eyes! Fufufu.

[]: Re: Unit-00's eye(s)             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Feb 10, 2014 9:39 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Maybe 00 really does have two eyes and the big mechanical thing is just feeding data.
